meta.description: A high-energy party game night where most players are all-in on chaos. Events fire frequently and the instigator is in full effect.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Ooh, Pat's going in! Okay, make your case—what'd you catch?
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly what I'm here for, let's see if Pat actually has something or if they're just being bold.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay — I caught Alex checking their phone when we were explaining the rules thirty seconds ago. Clean catch, I'm pitching it.
pat.internal_thought: This is a legit claim and a solid way to kick things off — establishes that the missions are real and people need to stay present. I should sell it but not oversell it since we're early.

sam.dialogue: Oh, um—yeah, I saw that too, I think? They were definitely on their phone for a second there.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, is Pat right? I think I saw someone check their phone but I'm not totally sure who or when—should I look confident or admit I'm not certain? Everyone else seems to know how this works.

marcus.dialogue: Alright Pat, what'd you catch? Make it quick.
marcus.internal_thought: It's early, people are barely settled, but I'm listening—this better be legit or I'm calling BS.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on — did I actually catch that or am I just claiming to look sharp? Let me think... yeah, no, I'm pretty sure that happened. Anyway, who's bidding?
pat.internal_thought: I'm claiming my own catch which feels a little self-serving for a host, but it was legit and I need to stay fair about this or people will call me out for favoritism.

sam.dialogue: Oh, um... wait, did Pat actually tell that story earlier? I'm still kind of figuring out who said what.
sam.internal_thought: I'm not confident enough to bid against anyone on this yet — I don't want to waste points on something I'm not sure about, and I definitely don't want to be the one blocking Pat if they're right.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—what story did Pat repeat? I need to actually hear it before I'm bidding points on this.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been talking a lot, so maybe he did repeat something, but I'm not letting him skate on a weak claim just because he's ahead—if the evidence is thin, I'm calling bullshit and keeping my points.

timeline.title: Say It Loud
timeline.description: First person to yell 'CHAOS AGENT' wins!

alex.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! *stands up* Yes, absolutely yes, this is the energy we needed right now.
alex.internal_thought: Oh this is perfect — I'm screaming it before anyone even processes what's happening, and now everyone's gonna look at me like I'm insane, which is exactly the vibe I want to establish for the whole night.

jade.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! CHAOS AGENT! Oh my god, did I—wait, did anyone else—?! That was so dumb, I love this game!
jade.internal_thought: THIS is exactly why I'm here, holy shit, let's GO.

pat.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! Okay, okay—I had to commit, and I'm taking that win. Everyone still with me? Energy's good, right?
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing—it's early enough that yelling breaks the ice without anyone being tired yet, and I needed a clean win to model the vibe I want: loud, fun, zero stakes.

tyler.internal_thought: I'm pretty sure someone just yelled something but I was looking at my phone and didn't catch it, and now I'm not gonna yell random stuff and look stupid.

sam.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! Wait—did I—? Did I win that?
sam.internal_thought: Oh that's actually fun and simple, I can do this, I don't have to think too hard about whether someone really said something or not.

marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of random noise that derails focus—we're in setup, I need to think about opening strategy, not yell like a toddler.

votingMechanic.name: Russian Roulette
votingMechanic.description: No vote. The chaos gods decide. 50/50.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's already hunting! *leans forward* This is what I'm talking about — we're eight minutes in and the predatory energy is IMMACULATE. Russian Roulette on an apology catch? The chaos gods are *chef's kiss*.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's moving fast and aggressive, which I respect, but now I'm gonna be hypervigilant for anyone apologizing so I can snipe that claim — this is exactly the kind of arms race I love to fuel.

jade.dialogue: Oh PAT! Yes, okay, I'm so here for this—did you actually catch someone apologizing?! This is gold, the chaos gods better come through!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's on a ROLL and I'm here for it, Russian roulette is insane, let's see if they pull it off—this is exactly the energy we need right now.

pat.dialogue: Oh, hell yeah—I caught that one fair and square. Marcus apologized to Tyler like thirty seconds ago for bumping his elbow, totally unprompted. That's the mission right there.
pat.internal_thought: Marcus is at 5.9 energy and already annoyed—giving him a win here (even if the RNG flips it) keeps him invested and reminds him he's part of the action, which is exactly what he needs right now.

marcus.dialogue: Pat's claiming 'The Apologizer' again? What'd you actually catch—did someone say sorry, or are we just gambling here?
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been hunting easy points all night and this Russian Roulette thing means I can't even scrutinize the claim properly—it's just luck, which is exactly the kind of chaos I hate.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: YES! Okay Pat, you're on a ROLL tonight—what'd you catch?! Was it legit? Tell us everything!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely dominating and I'm so here for it, this is exactly the chaos I came for.

pat.dialogue: Oh snap, yeah—I totally caught that! Jade said 'um' like twice when explaining her strategy before we even dealt the cards. That's a legit grab.
pat.internal_thought: Easy win, clean claim, and it keeps the momentum rolling without drama—plus I'm already ahead and want to keep things tight so Marcus doesn't feel completely buried.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat said 'um'? When? Like just now or... I wasn't really listening.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's probably right about something but I genuinely have no idea what was said two minutes ago, so I'll just vote whatever vibe feels right.

sam.dialogue: Oh, um—Pat definitely said 'um' like twice in that last thing, right? Yeah, I think that's legit.
sam.internal_thought: Pat's been loud and confident all night so I believe they caught someone actually saying it, and voting 'legit' is safer than looking like I'm being difficult on my first game night.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold up—did someone actually say 'um' or is Pat just fishing again? I wasn't tracking every word that hard.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been throwing out claims left and right and I'm starting to not trust any of them, but 'um' is easy to verify if it actually happened.

timeline.title: Host Power: target_player
timeline.description: Pat turns to Tyler: "Hey, you've been quiet -- what do you think? Pull them into the next event."

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait — Jade just *claimed* Potty Mouth? I didn't even catch what they swore on! *grins* Okay, so who's the Dictator tonight? Because if I'm voting, this is getting *interesting*.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect — Jade's already hunting, Pat's crushing it, and now we've got a Dictator vote on a claim nobody else verified yet. The setup is *chef's kiss* chaos waiting to happen.

jade.dialogue: Wait, I claimed that?! Oh my god, YES—okay so like two minutes ago Marcus said 'what the hell' and I'm pretty sure I called it in my head but I wasn't sure if anyone else heard it, so is that legit or am I reaching?
jade.internal_thought: Oh this is fun, I'm in the mix now, but also I hope Marcus doesn't feel called out because that would suck—who's the dictator on this one? Please let it be someone cool about it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—did you actually catch someone swearing, or are you just fishing? Walk me through it.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's energy is sky-high and she's loving the game, but I need to make sure this claim is legit before I rubber-stamp it—the Dictator mechanic means my word is final, and I can't let the early game get sloppy or people will stop trusting the system.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade got someone swearing? Sick. Wait, who's the dictator this round? That's actually wild.
tyler.internal_thought: I caught that Jade was hyped about something but missed when the swearing happened — was it recent? Doesn't matter, someone decides it anyway.

sam.dialogue: Um... did Jade actually say something with a swear word? I feel like I wasn't really... I mean, I don't think I caught that.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, was Jade swearing? I've been kind of zoned in on the game and not really listening to what everyone's saying—shit, am I supposed to have been? And who's 'The Dictator'? Why do I feel like I'm missing something obvious here.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually caught someone swearing? I wasn't even listening for that one. Who was it?
marcus.internal_thought: Jade's been riding high all night, but this one I need to actually verify before I'm handing over points—Pat's been throwing out bogus claims left and right.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.dialogue: Oh WAIT, hold up — Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? I'm absolutely volunteering for tribunal, because I need to know the *exact* glasses we're talking about here.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect — Jade's riding high and I can either make this messy or let it through, but either way I'm the one steering how hard we scrutinize it.

jade.dialogue: Wait, WAIT—I caught that?! Okay so like, I'm pretty sure Sam and Marcus both went for their drinks at the same time like two minutes ago, right? I literally just saw it happen and my brain was like 'ooh, is that the one?' and apparently I wasn't hallucinating!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god, YES—I actually nailed something! This is perfect, I love this, the jury is gonna have so much to deliberate on and honestly it doesn't even matter if I win because watching everyone figure this out together is the whole reason I'm here.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—I saw that happen like two minutes ago, you and Sam both went for your glasses at the exact same time, right? I'm jumping in as tribunal because I need to actually witness this one myself before we call it solid.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's on fire tonight and the energy's perfect—this claim is legit and it'll keep her momentum going, plus volunteering as judge keeps me in the flow without slowing the pace.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade got one? Wait, did I miss when that happened? I'll judge, I guess — sounds easy enough.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's actually crushing it tonight, and this one's straightforward — just gotta remember if I saw two people drink at the same time, which I probably didn't but whatever, let's see what she says.

sam.dialogue: Um... I mean, I didn't really see that happen? But like, I guess someone could've been drinking at the same time as someone else... I don't know if I should volunteer to judge though, I feel like I'd miss something.
sam.internal_thought: Why does everyone keep jumping on these things so fast? I literally just watched the game and didn't notice Jade drinking at the same time as anyone—but what do I know, maybe I blinked. Alex and Pat are so hyped and I'm still at zero points. Do I even want to volunteer to be a judge? That feels like a lot of pressure when I'm already confused about half the rules.

marcus.dialogue: Did you actually *see* that happen, or are we just claiming every time someone takes a sip? Because I wasn't tracking synchronized drinking.
marcus.internal_thought: Jesus, another claim right when I'm trying to focus on actual strategy—and honestly, Jade catching synchronized drinks? That's basically random luck, not skill, and it's pulling everyone's attention away from the real game.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

alex.dialogue: Oh PERFECT — Jade's going for The Interrupter? *leans back with a grin* Okay, so what's the bribe situation here? Because if you're offering me points to stay quiet, we're about to see if this table has a price on its chaos.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of moment I live for — Jade caught someone interrupting, now they're trying to buy me off, and I get to decide if the mechanic holds or if I blow it up by voting bullshit.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, wait—did I actually catch someone?! Okay so like, who was it—was it Tyler when he cut off Alex like five minutes ago? Oh wait, no—MARCUS! When Sam was talking about their score and Marcus just jumped in with that thing! Tell me I'm right, tell me I'm RIGHT!
jade.internal_thought: YESSS this is literally the best part of the game, I get to call out the chaos I've been watching unfold and EVERYONE knows I've been locked in the whole time.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade, you actually caught me? When? I don't remember that happening... okay, what's the bribe situation here?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's riding high and this is a legit catch, but I was locked in and didn't see it clearly—I need to know what she's offering before I decide, because I'm also thinking about keeping Marcus and Sam engaged since they're both tanking right now.

tyler.dialogue: Okay wait, Jade's claiming she caught someone interrupting? That's actually legit — I think I saw that happen like two minutes ago. What's the bribe?
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, was it me who got interrupted? Or did I interrupt someone? Actually doesn't matter — Jade's on a roll and this is one I can actually remember, so I should probably vote on this one.

sam.dialogue: Um... wait, did Jade actually interrupt someone? I feel like I wasn't totally paying attention to like... who was talking when? I don't really want to mess this up.
sam.internal_thought: I was just getting into the game and now I have to evaluate something I only half-watched while also trying not to look like I don't know what's going on.

marcus.dialogue: Are you *kidding* me right now? Jade, I was literally in the middle of my turn. Can we not do this every ninety seconds?
marcus.internal_thought: I'm down 45 points, I'm tired, I finally had a clean moment to think strategically, and now I have to stop and referee whether she actually caught someone interrupting or if this is another BS claim like the drinking thing.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Unanimous or Bust
votingMechanic.description: ONE bullshit call and it's over. All or nothing.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Okay okay okay — Pat's going for Name Dropper? *sits forward with a predatory smile* I need the EXACT moment, because I've been listening for celebrity mentions all night and my memory is *dangerous right now*.
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT — Pat's at 45 points, I'm at 20, and if I can torpedo this claim it shifts the entire power dynamic. Unanimous or bust means one bullshit call from anyone kills it. I'm about to make this *so much more complicated*.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa—hold up. I'm calling bullshit on myself here because this is exactly the kind of thing that tanks the vibe. Nobody heard me name-drop anything. I'm not doing this.
pat.internal_thought: I'm leading by 25 points in a tense endgame, Marcus is checked out and frustrated, Sam's energy is already dropping, and if I pull off an obviously fake claim when I'm winning, I'll look like a snake and kill the trust I've built all night—plus I'll confirm that claims are just free points if you're confident enough to lie, which ruins the whole game.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat name-dropped someone? Okay, I actually remember that — he was talking about some celebrity like three minutes ago. Yeah, I'm in.
tyler.internal_thought: Okay, this one I actually caught because I was locked in on the game moment and Pat's voice cut through — he definitely said a famous person's name. This is legit. Plus the game is tight right now and I want to actually be part of the decision.

sam.dialogue: Um... Pat did mention something about, like, a movie star earlier, right? I think? But I'm not totally sure if that counts or if I'm just... I don't know, making stuff up in my head.
sam.internal_thought: Pat's been on a roll all night and everyone loves them, so calling bullshit feels like a huge risk — but I also genuinely can't remember if they actually name-dropped someone or if I just blanked during the chaos.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Say It Loud
highlights.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! *stands up* Yes, absolutely yes, this is the energy we needed right now.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Oh WAIT, hold up — Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? I'm absolutely volunteering for tribunal, because I need to know the *exact* glasses we're talking about here.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Oh PERFECT — Jade's going for The Interrupter? *leans back with a grin* Okay, so what's the bribe situation here? Because if you're offering me points to stay quiet, we're about to see if this table has a price on its chaos.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait wait wait — Jade just *claimed* Potty Mouth? I didn't even catch what they swore on! *grins* Okay, so who's the Dictator tonight? Because if I'm voting, this is getting *interesting*.

lowPoints.title: Say It Loud
lowPoints.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! *stands up* Yes, absolutely yes, this is the energy we needed right now.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's already hunting! *leans forward* This is what I'm talking about — we're eight minutes in and the predatory energy is IMMACULATE. Russian Roulette on an apology catch? The chaos gods are *chef's kiss*.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only 14 chaos events in two hours felt weirdly restrained — I was expecting more opportunities to weaponize loopholes.
assessment.best_moment: When I called tribunal on Jade's Drink Spotter claim and got to psychoanalyze whether she was actually tracking glasses or just vibing — that's the moment the table realized no claim was safe.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly what I needed: two hours of pure theatrical chaos where every claim became a referendum and I got to turn the table into a jury box. Started hot at minute seven and never cooled down — my energy went from 7.1 to 9.9 because the game actively rewarded paranoia and strategic bullshitting. Finished second place, which I don't care about, but I absolutely dominated the actual game everyone will remember: turning Jade's claims into a stand-up comedy roast. The Chaos Agent mechanic was made for me.
suggestions: Add more flash missions — 14 events in 120 minutes is a drought when you're built for chaos
Let the Chaos Agent proposal votes or trigger secondary chaos — something to expand my actual power beyond disruption
Create claim conflict events where two people claim the same mission and the whole table has to duke it out
Add a 'Chaos Escalation' timer that cranks up event frequency if things get too calm
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I questioned Jade's Drink Spotter claim so aggressively that Pat literally had to mediate, and then Tyler started offering me a bribe to stay quiet — I created an entire subplot about whether I could be bought off, and the table spent ten minutes debating my price point. That's the night everyone's retelling.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Say It Loud
highlights.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! CHAOS AGENT! Oh my god, did I—wait, did anyone else—?! That was so dumb, I love this game!

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: YES! Okay Pat, you're on a ROLL tonight—what'd you catch?! Was it legit? Tell us everything!

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, wait—did I actually catch someone?! Okay so like, who was it—was it Tyler when he cut off Alex like five minutes ago? Oh wait, no—MARCUS! When Sam was talking about their score and Marcus just jumped in with that thing! Tell me I'm right, tell me I'm RIGHT!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, I claimed that?! Oh my god, YES—okay so like two minutes ago Marcus said 'what the hell' and I'm pretty sure I called it in my head but I wasn't sure if anyone else heard it, so is that legit or am I reaching?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, Pat's going in! Okay, make your case—what'd you catch?

lowPoints.title: Say It Loud
lowPoints.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! CHAOS AGENT! Oh my god, did I—wait, did anyone else—?! That was so dumb, I love this game!

assessment.biggest_complaint: I second-guessed myself on that Potty Mouth claim and totally killed my own momentum for like thirty seconds.
assessment.best_moment: When I caught Marcus interrupting Sam and just LOST IT trying to figure out if I was right—everyone was dying laughing at how badly I was explaining it.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was literally perfect. Two hours of pure chaos and I was here for EVERY second of it—didn't skip a single event because honestly, why would I? Watching Pat absolutely dominate the claims, seeing Marcus get caught mid-interrupt, hearing everyone's voices get louder and louder as we figured stuff out—that's the whole point, you know? I finished second but I'm pretty sure I won the night just by how much we were all laughing.
suggestions: Maybe give me a little visual confirmation when I've engaged with an event so I don't zone out wondering if it went through
Could we have a replay feature to watch the funniest moments back? I want to remember exactly what Marcus said when he got called out
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Okay so I caught Marcus interrupting Sam but I was SO unsure about it that I just started yelling 'TELL ME I'M RIGHT, TELL ME I'M RIGHT' at the group like a maniac, and everyone started chanting it back at me. It was completely unhinged and I loved every second of it.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Say It Loud
highlights.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! Okay, okay—I had to commit, and I'm taking that win. Everyone still with me? Energy's good, right?

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade—did you actually catch someone swearing, or are you just fishing? Walk me through it.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade—I saw that happen like two minutes ago, you and Sam both went for your glasses at the exact same time, right? I'm jumping in as tribunal because I need to actually witness this one myself before we call it solid.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa—hold up. I'm calling bullshit on myself here because this is exactly the kind of thing that tanks the vibe. Nobody heard me name-drop anything. I'm not doing this.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold on — did I actually catch that or am I just claiming to look sharp? Let me think... yeah, no, I'm pretty sure that happened. Anyway, who's bidding?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, hell yeah—I caught that one fair and square. Marcus apologized to Tyler like thirty seconds ago for bumping his elbow, totally unprompted. That's the mission right there.

assessment.biggest_complaint: That minute 75 situation where I had to call myself out nearly killed the vibe—false claims are poison for group trust, and I won't let that slide even if it's me.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 45 when I actually witnessed Jade and Sam both reaching for their drinks at the same time and got to play tribunal—that's the sweet spot where I'm engaged in the chaos without feeling manipulated.
assessment.narrative_summary: Tonight was exactly the kind of night I host for—the kind people text about the next day. The chaos agent layer kept everyone sharp and suspicious of each other without tearing the group apart, which is the whole trick. I ended up winning on the scoreboard, but that almost doesn't matter because everyone left energized and still friends. The only moment that soured me was that Name Dropper claim at minute 75 where I had to shut down my own potential point because the integrity of the night matters more than my rank.
suggestions: Flash missions are hitting too fast—either slow down their frequency or make them slightly harder to claim so people don't start gaming the system
Add a built-in 'tribunal mode' option where the group votes on disputed claims before points lock in, because that's where the real fun happened for me tonight
Let the host have a veto card or two to use on obviously bad-faith claims—not to cheat, but to protect the vibe when it matters
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I called out Jade on the 'Potty Mouth' claim at minute 32 and asked her to walk me through it—the way she tried to backpedal was comedy gold, and everyone instantly knew she was fishing. That's the moment where chaos agent stops being a game and becomes group psychology theater.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat name-dropped someone? Okay, I actually remember that — he was talking about some celebrity like three minutes ago. Yeah, I'm in.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Jade got one? Wait, did I miss when that happened? I'll judge, I guess — sounds easy enough.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Okay wait, Jade's claiming she caught someone interrupting? That's actually legit — I think I saw that happen like two minutes ago. What's the bribe?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Jade got someone swearing? Sick. Wait, who's the dictator this round? That's actually wild.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Jade got one? Wait, did I miss when that happened? I'll judge, I guess — sounds easy enough.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I missed like half the stuff happening because I wasn't paying attention, which made it hard to actually judge claims when my turn came up.
assessment.best_moment: When I actually caught Pat name-dropping that celebrity and got to call him out on it — felt like I was actually part of the game instead of just scrolling.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, I had a good time but I was kind of checked out for a lot of it. Started strong, got distracted around the middle, then actually got into it again toward the end when I started catching some of the claims people were making. The whole chaos thing with the AI dropping challenges was cool, but I definitely missed some of the key moments because I was on my phone. Still ended up having fun though, especially when I actually paid attention.
suggestions: Maybe make it louder or more obvious when something is about to happen so I actually notice instead of missing it
Could use a quick recap before claims so people like me who spaced out know what actually happened
The standing missions were way easier to track than the flash ones — more of those would help
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat getting called out for name-dropping that celebrity — he got all defensive about it and tried to deny it, but I literally remembered hearing it like two minutes before. His face was hilarious.

agentNarratives.name: Sam
agentNarratives.personality: Sam is at their first game night with this group and still figuring out the vibe. They laugh at jokes a beat too late, hesitate before voting, and get visibly overwhelmed when multiple events fire in quick succession. After three or more events within ten minutes their engagement drops sharply -- they'll go quiet, stop checking the app, and just watch until the pace calms down. They want to belong but don't want to look foolish trying.

highlights.title: Say It Loud
highlights.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! Wait—did I—? Did I win that?

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Oh, um—yeah, I saw that too, I think? They were definitely on their phone for a second there.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Oh, um—Pat definitely said 'um' like twice in that last thing, right? Yeah, I think that's legit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Um... wait, did Jade actually interrupt someone? I feel like I wasn't totally paying attention to like... who was talking when? I don't really want to mess this up.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Um... I mean, I didn't really see that happen? But like, I guess someone could've been drinking at the same time as someone else... I don't know if I should volunteer to judge though, I feel like I'd miss something.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Um... Pat did mention something about, like, a movie star earlier, right? I think? But I'm not totally sure if that counts or if I'm just... I don't know, making stuff up in my head.

assessment.biggest_complaint: There were too many judgment calls happening all at once and I couldn't keep up without feeling like I was going to mess up or miss something important.
assessment.best_moment: That first 'Say It Loud' thing where I actually got what was happening and felt like I was part of the chaos instead of just watching it.
assessment.narrative_summary: First game night with this group was... a lot? Started off feeling fun and included, but then things got really overwhelming around the middle when there were like a million things to judge and decide about all at once. I couldn't tell what was actually happening versus what I was imagining, so I mostly just shut down for a bit. By the end I was having more fun again, but I'm still not totally sure I understand all the rules or if I made the right calls.
suggestions: Maybe space out the judgment things more so people have time to actually pay attention and remember what happened
Make it clearer what counts as a 'real' claim versus like, someone just being really sure about something that might not have happened
Could use a quick recap of what happened during the event so everyone's on the same page before voting
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I think it was when I confidently said Pat said 'um' twice and everyone just looked at me, and then it turned out I was actually right? I was so relieved and everyone laughed because I sounded so unsure about something I was actually correct about.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—what story did Pat repeat? I need to actually hear it before I'm bidding points on this.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually caught someone swearing? I wasn't even listening for that one. Who was it?

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Alright Pat, what'd you catch? Make it quick.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you *kidding* me right now? Jade, I was literally in the middle of my turn. Can we not do this every ninety seconds?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Did you actually *see* that happen, or are we just claiming every time someone takes a sip? Because I wasn't tracking synchronized drinking.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The chaos agent interrupted critical moments constantly—I came dead last because I couldn't focus on actual strategy with someone derailing my turn every 90 seconds.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade actually caught someone swearing without it being a manufactured moment—that was the only interrupt that felt organic and earned.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong but the party game format paired with relentless chaos events turned the whole thing into a frustration factory. I finished 4th out of 6, which stung, but worse was feeling like I couldn't execute any actual strategy because someone was constantly pulling focus with manufactured claims. By minute 60 I was genuinely angry, and no amount of recovery at the end could salvage what felt like a rigged night.
suggestions: Stop interrupting active turns—claims should only trigger during downtime between rounds
Make the chaos events actually difficult to achieve, not just random accusations that disrupt play
Cut the frequency in half—14 events in 120 minutes is chaos spam, not enhancement
If you're running a competitive game, don't make chaos about blocking strategy execution
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade's genuine catch on someone swearing—she actually was paying attention instead of the AI just making wild claims, and everyone knew it actually happened. That's the only moment that landed because it wasn't manufactured.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
